title: SentinelMesh AI cybersecurity SaaS product video: brand-graded demo cut with 30s promo + 15s teaser cutdowns and on-brand callout / end-card graphic plates

source.reference: Video Editor & Motion Graphics Designer for AI Cybersecurity SaaS Product Video

grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Upwork brief: "an experienced video editor and motion graphics designer to create a polished marketing and product demonstration video for our AI-powered cybersecurity platform"; the product "helps organizations reduce human cyber risk through a combination of real-time protection, adaptive simulations, and in-the-moment learning"; "The video will be used on our website, sales presentations, webinars, investor discussions, and social media"; deliverables = "One polished 90-120 second marketing and product demonstration video", "One 30-second promotional cutdown", "One 15-second teaser". REASONABLY ENRICHED for a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark and tagged honestly: the real company becomes fictional IP-clean brand SentinelMesh (no real trademark/proprietary data); the open "product recordings + screenshots + messaging" bundle is fixed to concrete inputs (two 1080p screen recordings, a product b-roll clip, a flat logo PNG, a neutral end-card still, a brand color-reference frame, a messaging CSV); the three video deliverables are produced by the [A] async Quick Cut / reframe tools; and because the connector cannot composite motion graphics onto video, the brief's "animated callouts / captions" are delivered as standalone on-brand graphic PLATES (lower-third, call-out badge, vector logo SVG, graded end-card) the editor drops onto the Premiere timeline — exactly the "editable assets" the brief lists. No generative fill, text-to-image, object removal, upscaling, video-trim-to-timestamp, or compositing is claimed; image_fill_area is solid-color only; video_resize is same-length reframe.
one_line_ask: Turn SentinelMesh's raw screen recordings, product b-roll, logo, and brand color reference into a polished 90-120s AI-cybersecurity SaaS demo cut plus a 30s promo cutdown and 15s teaser (with a vertical social reframe), and build on-brand lower-third / call-out / graded end-card graphic plates and a vector logo as the editable-asset set for the Premiere handoff.
full_brief:
SentinelMesh is a B2B AI-powered cybersecurity platform that helps organizations reduce human cyber risk through real-time protection, adaptive phishing simulations, and in-the-moment learning nudges. We're preparing our next funding round and a website refresh, and we need a polished marketing and product-demonstration video plus social cutdowns. The finished pieces will run on our website hero, in sales decks, webinars, investor discussions, and across LinkedIn / X. We already have a strong foundation of material and want an editor to turn it into a professional, modern B2B SaaS product video (product footage + motion graphics + animated callouts + captions and visual storytelling — NOT a cartoon-style explainer).

Source material we're supplying: (1) two 1080p screen-capture recordings of the SentinelMesh console — the real-time threat dashboard and the adaptive-simulation builder; (2) a short b-roll clip of the product running on a laptop in a modern office; (3) our logo as a flat PNG; (4) a neutral, ungraded end-card hero still with negative space for a logo lockup; (5) a single brand color-reference frame representing the cool, confident, dark-mode look we want the piece graded toward; and (6) a messaging / talking-points CSV distilled from our whitepapers and website copy (the key claims, in priority order).

We need the freelancer to handle the full edit plus the supporting graphics, mapped to Adobe Creative Cloud connector tools with every step honestly tagged for execution mode:

A. VIDEO — (1) summarize the raw recordings + b-roll so we agree on the key talking points and a logical flow before cutting; (2) produce the primary deliverable: one polished 90-120 second product-demo cut that surfaces the priority messaging; (3) produce a 30-second promotional cutdown and a 15-second teaser, each as its own tighter pass (repurposed from the same footage, not exact-timestamp trims); (4) reframe the 15-second teaser to a 9:16 vertical version for social, same length (no trimming).

B. BRAND END-CARD + LOOK — from the neutral end-card hero still, build the graded end-card the video closes on and that also locks the brand look the editor matches the master grade to: straighten, auto-tone baseline, push the cool dark-mode color temperature toward the brand reference, shape vibrance/saturation, and crop/resize clean to 1920x1080.

C. GRAPHIC PLATES (standalone PNG / SVG swatch-and-position references, NOT composited onto the video) — the on-brand overlay references the editor rebuilds/lays onto the timeline: (1) vectorize the logo to a clean SVG for the end-card and corner bug; (2) a lower-third bar plate — a solid brand-color bar produced by selecting a region on the end-card canvas and solid-filling it (an opaque swatch/position reference, not a transparent cutout); (3) a call-out badge plate built the same way for on-screen fact call-outs; (4) an on-brand end-card variant with a subtle brand color overlay for the social teaser close.

Deliverables on approval: one 90-120s 1080p H.264 master demo; a 30s promo cutdown and a 15s teaser; a 9:16 vertical reframe of the teaser; one graded 1920x1080 end-card frame; logo SVG; lower-third and call-out PNG plates; and a social end-card variant, together forming the editable-asset set for the Premiere project handoff. Two rounds of feedback should be plenty.

inputs.name: screen_rec_threat_dashboard.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: 1080p screen-capture recording of a dark-mode SaaS cybersecurity console called 'SentinelMesh' — a real-time threat dashboard: a live risk-score gauge, a map of flagged phishing attempts lighting up, a table of at-risk employees, and a cursor moving to click through a threat detail panel. Flat un-graded screen-capture look, slight cursor lag, native UI, no cinematic color, ~40s representative clip. Deep charcoal background (#12151A) with a teal accent, crisp white type.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like a genuine OBS/Loom screen capture (flat exposure, real cursor motion, UI chrome), not a cinematic render, so the Quick Cut / summarize steps have real product footage to work from. Dark-mode teal palette matches the brand look the still is graded toward.

inputs.name: screen_rec_simulation_builder.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: 1080p screen-capture recording of the same 'SentinelMesh' dark-mode console showing the adaptive phishing-simulation builder: dragging a simulated phishing email template into a campaign, setting an audience segment, and a preview of an in-the-moment learning nudge popping up for an employee. Flat screen-capture look, native UI, cursor visible, ~35s. Same charcoal/teal dark-mode palette.
inputs.realism_notes: Second product-console recording so Quick Cut has two feature stories (protection + simulation) to weave; deliberately un-graded UI capture.

inputs.name: broll_product_on_laptop.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: 1080p b-roll: a laptop on a modern minimalist office desk running the SentinelMesh dark-mode dashboard, shallow depth of field, a professional in soft focus behind it, cool daylight through a window, gentle handheld drift. Neutral ungraded color, slightly flat contrast straight off the camera.
inputs.realism_notes: Live-action product b-roll to intercut with the screen recordings; ungraded/flat so it reads as raw footage the editor shapes. Documentary realism, not glossy CGI.

inputs.name: sentinelmesh_logo.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat, clean vector-style B2B tech logo on a transparent background: a stylized interlocking-mesh shield mark beside the wordmark 'SentinelMesh' in a modern geometric sans-serif, single teal brand color (#1FB6A6) with a deep-charcoal accent. Crisp edges, no gradients, no photographic texture — suitable for vectorizing.
inputs.realism_notes: Flat solid-color logo with crisp edges so image_vectorize produces a clean SVG. Text/logo asset => gemini-3-pro-image.

inputs.name: endcard_hero_frame.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean medium-wide hero still suitable for a video end-card: a laptop showing the SentinelMesh dark-mode dashboard on a modern office desk, ample negative space on the right for a logo lockup, neutral ungraded color and flat contrast straight off the camera, slightly tilted handheld framing. Photoreal, cool daylight, real desk texture and reflections.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately tilted and ungraded/flat so auto_straighten + the grade chain have real work; right-side negative space leaves room for the vectorized logo plate. Photoreal => gpt-image-2.

inputs.name: brand_color_reference.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: A cool, confident dark-mode brand color-reference photograph: a moody modern security-operations room, deep charcoal and teal tones, crisp white UI glow on monitors, soft controlled contrast, subtle film-like grain. Represents the calm, premium dark-mode look the whole piece should be graded toward.
inputs.realism_notes: Provides a believable cool/dark-mode target for the color-temperature and vibrance grade steps on the end-card; photoreal => gpt-image-2. Used as a match reference, not a deliverable.

inputs.name: messaging_talking_points.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: CSV with columns priority,claim,proof_point holding the SentinelMesh key messaging distilled from whitepapers/website, e.g. 1,'Most breaches start with human error','92% of incidents involve a click'; 2,'Real-time protection watches every click',...; 3,'Adaptive simulations train the riskiest users first',...; 4,'In-the-moment coaching turns risk into defense',... (6-8 rows, priority-ordered).
inputs.realism_notes: Reads like a marketing lead's talking-points sheet; passed as free-text prompt steering into media_summarize / Quick Cut so the cut surfaces the priority messaging — NOT as exact-timestamp cut instructions.

outputs.name: sentinelmesh_demo_master_1080p.mp4
outputs.spec: Primary deliverable: polished 90-120s AI-cybersecurity product-demo cut assembled from the two screen recordings + b-roll, surfacing the priority messaging; 1920x1080 (H.264 target on export).

outputs.name: sentinelmesh_promo_cutdown_30s.mp4
outputs.spec: 30-second promotional cutdown produced as its own tighter Quick Cut pass from the same footage, 1920x1080 H.264 — repurposed, not an exact-timestamp trim of the master.

outputs.name: sentinelmesh_teaser_15s.mp4
outputs.spec: 15-second teaser produced as the tightest Quick Cut pass, 1920x1080 H.264, for social / top-of-funnel.

outputs.name: sentinelmesh_teaser_15s_vertical_9x16.mp4
outputs.spec: 9:16 (1080x1920) reframe of the 15s teaser, SAME length (no trimming), for LinkedIn / stories social placements.

outputs.name: graded_endcard_frame.jpg
outputs.spec: One graded 1920x1080 end-card hero frame: straightened, auto-toned, temperature/vibrance-graded toward the cool dark-mode brand reference, with right-side negative space for the logo lockup — also the locked look the editor matches the master grade to.

outputs.name: sentinelmesh_logo.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vectorized SVG of the SentinelMesh logo for scalable end-card and corner-bug use.

outputs.name: lower_third_bar_plate.png
outputs.spec: Opaque lower-third bar plate frame (1920x1080 PNG): a horizontal region of the graded end-card canvas solid-filled with brand teal (#1FB6A6) to give the editor an exact on-brand bar swatch/position reference to drop on the Premiere timeline (solid-color fill only, no text composited; NOT a transparent cutout — headless selection-fill paints a region on the existing opaque canvas).

outputs.name: callout_badge_plate.png
outputs.spec: Opaque call-out badge plate frame (1920x1080 PNG): a badge-shaped region of the same end-card canvas solid-filled with the deep-charcoal accent (#12151A) as an on-brand fact-call-out swatch/position reference, same selection-fill technique (solid-color fill only; NOT a transparent cutout).

outputs.name: social_endcard_variant.jpg
outputs.spec: On-brand end-card variant with a subtle brand color overlay for the social teaser close, derived from the graded end-card frame.

connector_workflow.note: Create a managed Creative Cloud project folder ('SentinelMesh_Product_Video') to hold every source + produced asset. Root of the deliverable package.
Initialize upload of the client-supplied source files (two screen recordings, b-roll clip, logo png, end-card still, brand color reference, messaging CSV) into project_folder.
Finalize the multipart uploads; returns stored CC asset handles for every source file — the chain's root inputs now live in the project.

Summarize the two screen recordings + b-roll (content/flow) so we agree on the key talking points and logical order before cutting; steered by the messaging CSV priorities. Consumes the ingested video assets. [A] async-widget.

Primary deliverable: AI Quick Cut of the recordings + b-roll into a polished 90-120s product-demo master, prompted by the agreed talking points from step 4 + messaging CSV. 1080p H.264. [A] async-widget.

Produce the 30-second promotional cutdown as its own tighter Quick Cut pass over the same footage (repurposed, NOT an exact-timestamp trim of the master), prompt-tightened to the top 2-3 messaging priorities. [A] async-widget.
Produce the 15-second teaser as the tightest Quick Cut pass, prompt-focused on the single hero claim + product beauty shot. [A] async-widget.
Reframe the 15s teaser to 9:16 vertical (1080x1920) for social — SAME length, no trimming. Consumes the step-7 teaser. [A] async-widget.
Level the deliberately-tilted end-card hero still — first step of the end-card / brand-look grade chain. Consumes the ingested end-card still. [C].
Lay down an auto-tone baseline on the straightened end-card before the brand-look grade. Consumes step-9. [C].
Shift the end-card's white balance toward the cool dark-mode look, matching the ingested brand color-reference frame. Consumes step-10 + the color reference. [C].
Shape vibrance/saturation so the teal UI accents pop against the charcoal for the confident dark-mode brand look. Consumes step-11. [C].

Crop/resize the graded end-card clean to 1920x1080, preserving right-side negative space for the logo lockup -> graded end-card frame deliverable (also the locked brand-look reference). Consumes step-12. [C].
Vectorize the flat logo PNG to a clean scalable SVG for the end-card and corner bug. Consumes the ingested logo. [C].
On a copy of the graded end-card frame reused as the (opaque) plate canvas, select a horizontal lower-third bar region. Consumes step-13. [C].

Solid-fill the selected region with brand teal (#1FB6A6) to produce the lower-third bar plate — an opaque 1920x1080 swatch/position reference (solid-color fill only, no text composited; NOT a transparent cutout, since headless selection-fill paints onto the existing opaque canvas). Consumes step-15. [C].
On the same plate canvas, select a rounded badge region for the on-screen fact call-out. Consumes step-16. [C].

Solid-fill the badge region with the deep-charcoal accent (#12151A) to produce the call-out badge plate — an opaque swatch/position reference (solid-color fill only; NOT a transparent cutout). Consumes step-17. [C].
Apply a subtle brand teal-charcoal color overlay to the graded end-card frame to produce the on-brand social teaser end-card variant. Consumes step-13. [C].

chaining_note: Strict output->input chain. Steps 1-3 stand up the project: create folder -> init upload -> finalize upload yields ingested_assets (every source file). Two sub-chains then fan out to produce the editable-asset set. (VIDEO [A]) media_summarize(4) consumes the ingested recordings + b-roll -> its footage_summary steers three Quick Cut passes: the 90-120s master(5), the 30s promo cutdown(6), and the 15s teaser(7), each consuming ingested_assets + footage_summary as free-text prompt steering (repurposed passes, NOT exact-timestamp trims); video_resize(8) then consumes the 15s teaser to produce the same-length 9:16 vertical reframe. (STILLS/PLATES [C]) a 5-step linear end-card grade threads the hero still through every link — auto_straighten(9)->apply_auto_tone(10)->adjust_color_temperature(11, also reading the ingested brand color reference as match target)->adjust_vibrance_and_saturation(12)->crop_and_resize(13)=graded_endcard_frame; image_vectorize(14) consumes the ingested logo -> logo SVG; the plates reuse the graded end-card as canvas: select_by_prompt(15)->fill_area(16)=lower-third bar -> its output feeds select_by_prompt(17)->fill_area(18)=call-out badge; apply_color_overlay(19) consumes the graded end-card -> social end-card variant. Nothing after step 3 starts from scratch; every step reads a prior step's named output (or the finalized ingest set for a legitimate sub-chain root).
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 19 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four connector groups (asset/storage, video [A], photo/raster grade [C], vector + solid-fill plates) with two parallel sub-chains that produce one Premiere-handoff editable-asset set — well past the >=12 calls / >=3 groups / >=6 distinct-tool flagship bar. Difficulty comes from (a) honoring the [A] async vs [C] headless split correctly — the four video deliverables (master, promo, teaser, vertical) are async-widget/product-doable, while the durable creative spine (5-link end-card grade, logo SVG, two selection->solid-fill plates, color-overlay variant) is fully headless-confirmed; (b) respecting the hard limits while still satisfying the brief's 'motion graphics / animated callouts / captions' intent — the connector does NOT composite text/graphics onto video, so the callouts, lower-third, and end-card are delivered as standalone graphic PLATES that the editor lays on the timeline; the three cutdowns are separate Quick Cut passes (NOT video-trim-to-timestamp); video_resize is same-length reframe only; image_fill_area is solid-color only; no generative fill, no text-to-image / from-scratch motion design, no AI object removal, no upscaling; (c) keeping every post-ingest step strictly output->input chained into a single coherent editable-asset package.

reverify.banned_capability_check: Confirmed NONE of the banned capabilities are used: no generative fill / generative-expand of new content; no canvas extension/padding (image_crop_and_resize at step 13 only crops/resizes the existing frame to 1920x1080, it does not enlarge the canvas); no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo/motion-graphics design; no upscaling / super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting; no bespoke font/letterform design; no unreachable external account. image_fill_area is solid-color fill only, painting a region onto the existing OPAQUE end-card canvas — the lower-third + call-out plates are opaque swatch/position references, NOT transparent cutouts (headless selection-fill cannot produce alpha transparency, so the plate outputs are honestly specified as opaque PNG frames). image_vectorize traces the whole flat logo raster -> SVG (no OCR / region-extraction claim). video_create_quick_cut is used as visual-engagement AI cutting driven by free-text messaging/summary steering (tool schema confirms visual-engagement metrics, NOT timestamp trimming) — the three cutdowns are independent tighter passes via distinct target_duration + user_prompt, not exact-timestamp trims of the master. video_resize is same-length 9:16 reframe (width/height only, no trimming). media_summarize is content/flow summarization of the recordings + b-roll (no beat/BPM/tempo claim). No text or graphics are composited onto video — all overlays are delivered as standalone PNG/SVG plates.
reverify.notes: Verified against tool schemas (video_create_quick_cut, video_resize, media_summarize) and against the acknowledged pattern parent AO-34. Every video step (4,5,6,7,8) is honestly tagged [A] async-widget (product-doable, not headless-retrievable); every still/plate/asset step is [C] headless-confirmed. The brand color-reference frame at step 11 is used purely as a grade match target (a supplied still), consistent with AO-34's honest pattern. All 19 steps server=='adobe'; no [W] Express-widget tools, no Canva tools, no [L] local step. 15 distinct Adobe tools (>=6), 19 calls (>=10) -> qualifies as long-horizon flagship. Counts verified: connector_workflow has 19 steps -> tool_call_count=19; video_create_quick_cut appears 3x but counts once, giving 15 distinct adobe-server tools = distinct_adobe_tools. Minor realism note (not a defect): media_summarize summarizes the visual content/flow of the UI screen captures + b-roll to produce the priority talking points that steer the cuts, framed as a talking-point/flow summary, matching AO-34's summary pattern and staying in-bounds. Slot parity confirmed: category=video_edit, operation=O3, family='Video & Motion', output_modality=video, workflow_nature=edit (transforms supplied footage/assets into edited deliverables), vertical=tech_software_saas. Grounded in the real Upwork 'AI Cybersecurity SaaS Product Video' brief with a fictional IP-clean brand (SentinelMesh).
